title: LateBind: Controlled Additive-Value Tests for Timing-Aware Shortcut Mitigation in Text Classification
description: LateBind is a diagnostic evaluation paper, not a method paper. It tests whether a risk-gated intermediate predictive-entropy term adds measurable value beyond masking-based regularization and generic debiasing for shortcut robustness, using a validation-clean protocol, stronger stress tests, and explicit proxy-validation analyses.
motivation: NLP shortcut-robustness gains are easy to overclaim, and recent mechanistic work suggests some failures arise because models commit to labels too early. Existing ingredients already cover masking consistency, entropy-based debiasing, counterfactual augmentation, and generic reweighting; the gap is a controlled additive-value test showing whether timing-aware regularization contributes anything beyond those ideas, including on at least one natural non-synthetic setting.
proposed_approach: Use `roberta-base` with auxiliary classifiers at two intermediate layers, train-only shortcut-risk estimation, a risk-gated predictive-entropy floor, and a MASKER-style final-layer masking-consistency loss. Evaluate ERM, MASKER, JTT, and LateBind on actor-correlated IMDb with validation-only model selection, actor-conflict and actor-scrubbed stress tests, then require a smaller CivilComments check to test whether any timing-aware signal survives in a natural spurious-correlation setting.

related_work.title: Identifying Spurious Correlations for Robust Text Classification
related_work.how_it_differs: This earlier robust text-classification paper explicitly identifies spurious lexical correlations and removes them to improve worst-case robustness. LateBind does not classify terms as spurious versus genuine; it asks whether premature intermediate confidence is an additive signal beyond token-focused mitigation.

related_work.title: Robustness to Spurious Correlations in Text Classification via Automatically Generated Counterfactuals
related_work.how_it_differs: This is a key counterfactual-augmentation predecessor: it generates counterfactual text using likely causal features and antonym substitution. LateBind does not create new training examples, and its novelty claim is restricted to the controlled additive-value test for timing-aware regularization plus proxy validation.

related_work.title: MASKER: Masked Keyword Regularization for Reliable Text Classification
related_work.authors: Seung Jun Moon, Sangwoo Mo, Kimin Lee, Jaeho Lee, Jinwoo Shin
related_work.how_it_differs: MASKER is the closest masking-based predecessor and a mandatory comparator. LateBind intentionally includes a masking-consistency term so the paper can test whether the timing term adds value beyond masking rather than claiming a distinct masking method.

related_work.title: Entropy-based Attention Regularization Frees Unintended Bias Mitigation from Lists
related_work.authors: Giuseppe Attanasio, Debora Nozza, Dirk Hovy, Elena Baralis
related_work.how_it_differs: EAR already shows that entropy-based regularization can mitigate lexical bias, so LateBind cannot claim entropy regularization itself as novel. The difference is predictive entropy instead of attention entropy, risk-gated instead of global regularization, and a diagnostic additive-value evaluation rather than a new debiasing claim.

related_work.title: Just Train Twice: Improving Group Robustness without Training Group Information
related_work.authors: Evan Zheran Liu, Behzad Haghgoo, Annie S. Chen, Aditi Raghunathan, Pang Wei Koh, Shiori Sagawa, Percy Liang, Chelsea Finn
related_work.how_it_differs: JTT is the key generic debiasing baseline. It tests whether simple error-focused reweighting already explains the robustness gain, which would weaken the need for shortcut-specific timing control.

related_work.title: Understanding and Mitigating Spurious Correlations in Text Classification with Neighborhood Analysis
related_work.authors: Oscar Chew, Hsuan-Tien Lin, Kai-Wei Chang, Kuan-Hao Huang
related_work.how_it_differs: NFL identifies spurious tokens through neighborhood analysis and mitigates representation misalignment. LateBind differs by focusing on additive value from intermediate commitment control rather than better token discovery.

related_work.title: Short-circuiting Shortcuts: Mechanistic Investigation of Shortcuts in Text Classification
related_work.authors: Leon Eshuijs, Shihan Wang, Antske Fokkens
related_work.how_it_differs: This paper motivates the timing hypothesis by showing that shortcut-related internal components can steer predictions early. LateBind does not claim mechanistic localization; it tests whether auxiliary-head predictive entropy is a useful cheap proxy with measurable additive training-time value.

related_work.title: Whispers of Doubt Amidst Echoes of Triumph in NLP Robustness
related_work.authors: Ashim Gupta, Rishanth Rajendhran, Nathan Stringham, Vivek Srikumar, Ana Marasovic
related_work.how_it_differs: This work motivates the stricter paper design: no test-driven tuning, narrow claims, stress tests beyond the main synthetic split, and explicit controls against generic confidence flattening.

hypothesis: If harmful shortcut reliance is partly expressed as premature low-entropy intermediate predictions on shortcut-prone examples, then a risk-gated late-commitment term should improve actor-correlated IMDb anti-correlated OOD performance over ERM and outperform at least one of MASKER or JTT, while remaining competitive on actor-conflict and actor-scrubbed stress tests and preserving clean accuracy and calibration.

confirm: LateBind improves over ERM on actor-correlated IMDb `test_ood` after all selection decisions are made using validation data only.
LateBind beats at least one strong comparator among MASKER and JTT on the primary benchmark.
LateBind remains competitive on the actor-conflict and actor-scrubbed stress tests, showing gains are not reducible to handling a single injected actor token.
No-shortcut IMDb accuracy and calibration do not materially worsen relative to ERM.
Low intermediate entropy on high-risk validation examples correlates with shortcut-mask sensitivity, even after controlling for actor presence and final confidence.
Risk-gated timing regularization outperforms an ungated predictive-entropy baseline, indicating the effect is selective rather than generic uncertainty inflation.
CivilComments shows at least a small worst-group F1 gain or a clearer early-commitment diagnostic signal than ERM.
refute: MASKER and JTT match or exceed LateBind once evaluation is fully validation-clean.
Any LateBind gain disappears on actor-conflict or actor-scrubbed splits, implying the effect mostly tracks the injected actor token.
The timing term improves OOD only by flattening confidence broadly, as shown by no-shortcut degradation or entropy inflation on low-risk examples.
Hybrid risk gating performs no better than random-token or actor-only controls.
Intermediate predictive entropy fails to track shortcut sensitivity after controlling for actor presence and raw confidence.
CivilComments shows no signal at all, suggesting the timing proxy is too benchmark-specific to support a stronger contribution.
